title: galarhoeus esula subsp. cyparissioides
intro: Galarhoeus esula subsp. cyparissioides, also known as the Caper Spurge, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is characterized by its unique, fleshy, and milky sap, which has been used for medicinal purposes for centuries. The plant's milky sap has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Galarhoeus esula subsp. cyparissioides can be prepared as a topical ointment or salve, and its sap can also be used to make herbal remedies such as tinctures and infusions.
